Weeknight Kitchen Recipes Pantry Newsletter

One-Pan Lemon Chicken, Step by Step

This is the recipe I hand to anyone who claims they cannot cook. One pan, one lemon, six steps, and a result that tastes like twice the effort. The only technique that matters is patience while the skin crisps; everything else forgives improvisation, substitution, and distraction.

  1. Pat four chicken thighs dry and salt them generously on both sides. Dry skin is the entire secret to crispness, so do not rush this step.
  2. Set a heavy oven-safe pan over medium heat, add a spoon of oil, and lay the thighs in skin-side down. Leave them alone for ten minutes.
  3. While they render, slice one lemon thin and smash four garlic cloves. Strip the leaves from a few thyme sprigs if you have them.
  4. Flip the thighs, tuck the lemon slices and garlic between them, and slide the pan into a hot oven for fifteen minutes.
  5. Rest the chicken on a board while you swirl a splash of stock into the pan juices to make a quick sauce.
  6. Serve over rice or bread, spoon the sauce generously, and eat while the skin still crackles.

Leftovers keep for three days and improve overnight as the lemon mellows into the fat. If you double the recipe, use two pans rather than crowding one; steam is the enemy of every step above.
